rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the most specific valid scope for the target component or implementation
Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use visible identifiers when possible to maintain traceability and avoid unresolved references
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` syntax for AGREE clauses
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
anti_patterns: Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
